description_long: This object is a vintage tabletop radio receiver with a rectangular wooden body and a polished, dark brown veneer. The front panel features a large, horizontally ribbed speaker grille, flanked by two round tuning and volume knobs, and a row of push buttons beneath a yellowed frequency indicator window. The top surface is smooth and houses a retractable metal antenna, which is extended in the images. Both sides of the radio have square, mesh-covered speaker vents, and one side includes a red switch or button. The back panel is plain, showing some wear and discoloration, typical of aged wood. The overall design is reminiscent of mid-20th-century radios, combining functional controls with a classic, decorative appearance.
description: A vintage wooden tabletop radio receiver with a ribbed front speaker grille, tuning knobs, push buttons, and an extended metal antenna. The sides feature mesh speaker vents and a red switch, while the back is plain and worn.
description_view_0: Front view showing the ribbed speaker grille, two round knobs, push buttons, and the yellow frequency indicator window.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the mesh speaker vent and the extended metal antenna mounted on top.
description_view_2: Rear view showing a plain, slightly worn wooden surface with the antenna visible on top.
description_view_3: Right side view with a mesh speaker vent and a prominent red switch or button on the top rear corner.
